UTSouthern Utah training

The coach

Built on live reps.

Nia Hagler coaches the details that show up when the match gets messy: hand fighting, angles, hips, pressure, chain attacks, and the reset after a hard exchange.

Sessions are direct and personal. You bring the goal, the problem position, or the next tournament. Nia brings the pace, corrections, and standards.

This is training for wrestlers who want to feel harder to score on and more dangerous when the match breaks open.

Train with Nia

Get mat ready.

Every session has a purpose: sharpen what is already working, fix the position costing points, and build a style that holds up when the pace jumps.

01 / Individual

Private Lessons

Focused technical coaching built around the athlete in front of her.

  • Position-specific technique
  • Live troubleshooting
  • Clear take-home cues
02 / Competition

Match Prep

Pressure-tested rounds and strategy for the next tournament or season.

  • Chain wrestling
  • Situational goes
  • Scoring under fatigue
03 / Mindset

Sports Psychology

Simple routines for competing calm, focused, and aggressive.

  • Pre-match preparation
  • Confidence under pressure
  • Post-match reset
Technique

Attack chains, finishes, riding pressure, defense, and mat returns.

Pace

Short, intense rounds that teach athletes to score when tired.

Confidence

Specific feedback so athletes know exactly what to trust in a match.

Match moments

Pressure. Finishes. Scrambles.

A tighter look at the positions that decide matches: pressure on top, committed finishes, and wrestling through the whistle.

Ready to work?

Policies

Clear expectations before training.

Privacy Policy

This website does not collect payment information, account logins, or form submissions. If you message Nia through Instagram, that conversation is handled by Instagram and its privacy practices.

  • Basic website traffic may be processed by the hosting provider to keep the site secure and working.
  • Contact details shared through Instagram DMs are used only to respond about training, scheduling, and availability.
  • Personal information is not sold by this website.
  • Photos and media on the site are used to show wrestling training, competition, and coaching style.

Last updated July 2026

Training Disclaimer

Wrestling and athletic training involve physical contact, intense movement, and risk of injury. Participation is voluntary, and athletes should only train if they are healthy enough for physical activity.

  • A parent or guardian should approve training for athletes under 18.
  • Training guidance is not medical advice and does not replace care from a doctor, trainer, or licensed professional.
  • Results are not guaranteed; progress depends on effort, consistency, health, and competition level.
  • Cancellation, refund, and session details should be confirmed directly with Nia before booking.

Last updated July 2026
